Group Category: Group 6: Weapons Violations and Public Safety Offenses

Collateral Employment: Absolute statutory bar from acting as a private security guard, corporate safety director, law enforcement asset, or any role involving public trust administration.

Jail Only: Yes (Sentences are served in local county jails/workhouses, not state prison).
Prison Eligible: No (Unless enhanced to a Class E felony due to specific repeat priors).

Work Release Eligible: Yes (Conditional upon local jail program availability and court authorization).

Offense Description: Executive Pardon — An act of executive clemency issued by the Governor that forgives a conviction and restores an individual's core civil and political rights.

Electronic Monitoring: No (Terminates active monitoring obligations unless explicitly restricted by executive conditions)

Sealing Waiting Period: Varies based on the underlying offense type.
Expungement Eligible: Yes (Conditional upon filing an expungement petition following the issuance of the pardon)
State Law Restorable Only (Restores state-level firearm rights, except for convictions involving violence, deadly weapons, or specific high-tier drug felonies)
Voting Rights Restored: Intact / Automatic (Restores core voting franchises upon formal presentation to local election commissions)
License Impact: Clears the absolute statutory bar for professional licenses; however, boards retain discretionary character evaluation authority.
Collateral Raw Summary: Restores core civil rights, including the right to vote, hold public office, and serve on a jury; however, the offense remains on historical record logs unless an expungement order is secured.
Collateral Firearms: Restores state handgun carrying rights, provided the underlying offense does not involve violence or deadly weapons.
Collateral Immigration: A state governor's pardon can waive specific deportation grounds under federal immigration law; however, it does not completely eliminate drug-related immigration penalties.
Collateral Voting: Automatically restores electoral eligibility upon presentation of the pardon documentation.
Collateral Housing: Significantly reduces risk by demonstrating official rehabilitation to housing authorities and corporate landlords.
Collateral Registry: No (The Governor rarely grants pardons for offenses that require sex offender registration)
Collateral Employment: Clears direct bars for government employment, public trust positions, and state procurement roles.

No (Permanent state and federal firearm ban)

Statutory Title: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Tennessee Uniform Administrative Procedures Act (UAPA)

Offense Description: Procedural mechanism to appeal an emergency summary license suspension enacted by a Department of Health Board upon a finding that public health, safety, or welfare imperatively requires emergency action.

Probation Eligible: No (The board may lift the suspension and replace it with a conditional stayed suspension/probationary practice monitor)
Mandatory Minimum: Statutory Time Limit: Board must launch formal contested case proceedings within seven (7) business days of the summary suspension order, or the emergency order automatically expires by operation of law
Sealing Eligible: No (Emergency summary suspensions and subsequent appellate orders are binding public health records with zero path to sealing)

Voting Rights Restored: Intact

Collateral Firearms: Intact
Collateral Immigration: Purely procedural appeals do not impact status under INA § 237
Collateral Housing: No direct private restriction
Collateral Registry: Temporary status is listed as "Suspended—Emergency Order" on the public health portal during the appeal process
Collateral Employment: Direct and immediate suspension of all active hospital staff privileges, private practice operations, and managed-care insurance billing networks until an appellate order stays or reverses the board's action.

Offense Description: GSA SAM System Exclusion Registration; the central, master administrative vehicle utilized to record, display, and execute procurement and non-procurement default exclusions across the entire federal government, ensuring that persons/entities listed are blocked from receiving federal grants, loans, benefits, or contract payouts.
Lifecycle Stage: Administrative Entry / Global Government Marketplace Exclusion
Act 659 / State Equiv: Yes (Functions as the absolute baseline screening tool utilized by state agencies to audit vendor eligibility for federal pass-through block grants)
Max Jail/Prison Time: None (Master registry tool)
Max Financial Fine: None independently (Triggers government-wide denial of all discretionary financial transfers, subsidies, and awards)
Mandatory Minimum: Durations match the specific underlying agency debarment code entered into the terminal database (ranging from temporary suspension holds to permanent lifetime statutory bars)
Sealing Eligible: No (Statutorily mandated under the Federal Funding Accountability and Transparency Act to remain completely open, searchable public data)
Expungement Eligible: No (Automated cleanup executes strictly on the exact date established by the initial setting agency's debarment order terminal entry)
Intact
Voting Rights Restored: Intact
License Impact: Absolute government-wide restriction blocking the target from receiving any direct or indirect federal financial assistance, commercial loans, or operational grants.
BTB Status: Excluded (Functions as the ultimate commercial background check anchor for public sector markets)
Collateral Raw Summary: Master federal registry entry that enforces an absolute bar on all federal grants, commercial contract bids, and public pass-through funding.
Collateral Firearms: Intact
Collateral Immigration: Low risk independently, unless linked to a base underlying passport or international trade fraud conviction
Collateral Housing: Severe restriction; blocks any corporate entity access to FHA commercial lending pipelines, federal redevelopment grants, or infrastructure subsidies
Collateral Registry: Absolute active status inside the GSA SAM.gov Exclusion Database
Collateral Employment: Total structural barrier from serving as an executive director, principal investor, key vendor, or grant administrator for any university, non-profit, or corporation utilizing public funding loops.

Group Category: Group 9: Administrative, Procurement, and Regulatory Exclusions
Statutory Title: Code of Federal Regulations (C.F.R.) / Department of Housing and Urban Development
Authority Type: Federal Agency Administrative Regulation 2 C.F.R. Part 2424
Record Type: Administrative Finding / Formal Housing Market Exclusion
Offense Description: HUD Non-Procurement Debarment; a formal administrative determination by the Department of Housing and Urban Development excluding a contractor, developer, or landlord from participating in HUD programs due to systemic violations of the Fair Housing Act, equity diversion, or mortgage fraud.
Internal Screening Tier: Tier 2 (Highly Severe commercial housing restriction tier)
Act 659 / State Equiv: Yes (Triggers reciprocal exclusions from state housing development authorities, such as the Tennessee Housing Development Agency)
Max Jail/Prison Time: None (Administrative remedy; criminal tracking requires separate Department of Justice prosecution under 18 U.S.C. § 1010)
Max Financial Fine: None independently under this part (Triggers complete termination of federal mortgage insurance, housing vouchers, and development grants)
Mandatory Minimum: Exclusions typically run for a standard baseline period of three (3) years under 2 C.F.R. § 180.865, but can be extended based on the severity of the equity diversion or fair housing non-compliance
Sealing Eligible: No (HUD debarment files are permanently recorded on searchable public federal databases to ensure transactional transparency)
Expungement Eligible: No (Removal requires a formal petition for reinstatement to the HUD Debarring Official demonstrating full operational remediation)
Pardon Eligible: No (Executive clemency does not apply to civil administrative exclusions)
Intact
Voting Rights Restored: Intact
License Impact: Complete immediate exclusion from participating in any FHA-insured mortgage programs, Section 8 voucher distributions, or HUD development grants; triggers rapid suitability reviews by state real estate and property management boards.
BTB Status: Excluded (Appears directly on commercial real estate credit profiles and federal lookup databases)
Consequence Severity: Tier 5 (Catastrophic commercial real estate and funding exclusion)
Collateral Raw Summary: HUD program debarment results in immediate termination of federal housing development grants, voucher distributions, and mortgage access.
Collateral Firearms: Intact
Collateral Immigration: Low risk independently
Collateral Housing: Absolute structural bar from receiving federal multi-family development loans or acting as a registered landlord for Section 8 properties
Collateral Registry: Mandatory active registration inside the GSA SAM.gov Exclusion Database and HUD's Limited Denial of Participation (LDP) list
Collateral Employment: Total lifetime barrier from serving as a principal officer, construction contractor, real estate broker, or property manager for any entity utilizing federal housing funds.

Group Category: Group 9: Administrative, Procurement, and Regulatory Exclusions
Statutory Title: Code of Federal Regulations (C.F.R.) / Department of Agriculture
Authority Type: Federal Agency Administrative Regulation 2 C.F.R. Part 417
Offense Description: USDA Non-Procurement Exclusion and SNAP Retailer Disqualification; an administrative determination by the Department of Agriculture excluding a commercial vendor, food processing entity, or retail store from federal agricultural programs due to food safety defaults, continuous inspection violations, or trafficking in Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) benefits.
Internal Screening Tier: Tier 2 (Highly Severe commercial agricultural and retail restriction tier)
Act 659 / State Equiv: Yes (Triggers automatic disqualification from participating in state-administered nutritional programs, such as WIC and local school lunch supply chains)
Max Jail/Prison Time: None (Administrative remedy; criminal metrics require separate Department of Justice prosecution under 7 U.S.C. § 2024)
Max Financial Fine: None independently under this part (Triggers absolute loss of SNAP EBT processing rights and eligibility for federal agricultural subsidies)
Mandatory Minimum: SNAP benefits trafficking or critical food safety fraud triggers a mandatory minimum of a five (5) year exclusion up to a permanent lifetime disqualification under 7 C.F.R. § 278.6(e)
Sealing Eligible: No (USDA exclusion files are published on public federal registries to protect the integrity of federal nutrition programs and public safety)
Expungement Eligible: No (Reversal requires formal administrative appeal via judicial review channels under 7 U.S.C. § 2023)
Intact
Voting Rights Restored: Intact
License Impact: Complete immediate termination of electronic benefit transfer (EBT) transaction authorizations; triggers discretionary review and suspension of local commercial health and food establishment operational permits.
BTB Status: Excluded (Appears directly on commercial merchant screening profiles)
Consequence Severity: Tier 5 (Catastrophic retail and food manufacturing market exclusion)
Collateral Raw Summary: USDA administrative exclusion results in immediate termination of SNAP EBT processing rights, agricultural subsidies, and wholesale food supply certifications.
Collateral Firearms: Intact
Collateral Immigration: Low risk independently, unless associated with large-scale border smuggling operations
Collateral Housing: Low risk
Collateral Registry: Mandatory active listing on the GSA SAM.gov Exclusion List and the USDA Food and Nutrition Service (FNS) Disqualified Retailer List
Collateral Employment: Total structural barrier from serving as a manager, owner, corporate officer, or majority partner for any wholesale grocery operation, commercial food processor, or retail outlet utilizing federal nutrition assistance loops.

Group Category: Group 9: Administrative, Procurement, and Regulatory Exclusions
Statutory Title: Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) / Defense Federal Acquisition Regulation Supplement (DFARS)
Offense Description: Corporate Procurement Fraud Debarment; an administrative exclusion of a corporation or defense contractor from bidding on or receiving public works awards, executed by an agency debarring official based on a preponderance of the evidence demonstrating systemic corporate fraud, billing inflation, product substitution, or defective pricing.
Lifecycle Stage: Administrative Entry / Active Corporate Debarment Execution
Internal Screening Tier: Tier 2 (Highly Severe corporate operational restriction tier)
Act 659 / State Equiv: Yes (Triggers automated corporate vendor blacklisting across reciprocal state procurement departments)
Max Jail/Prison Time: None under this part (Administrative track; individual criminal culpability requires separate prosecution under 18 U.S.C. § 1031)
Max Financial Fine: None independently under this part (Triggers total commercial termination of current and future public contract pipeline revenues)
Mandatory Minimum: Debarment for procurement fraud typically runs for a standard baseline period of three (3) years under 48 C.F.R. § 9.406-4, but can be extended to five (5) or more years if national security or military supply chain lines are compromised
Sealing Eligible: No (Corporate debarments are published immediately on open public federal systems to prevent public funds from being allocated to non-responsible entities)
Expungement Eligible: No (Removal requires an administrative petition showing full corporate restructuring, termination of culpable employees, and the installation of a certified compliance program)
Intact
Voting Rights Restored: Intact
License Impact: Complete corporate exclusion blocking the entity and all affiliates from bidding on, receiving, or administering any public contract, subcontract, or federally funded grant.
BTB Status: Excluded (Functions as a critical anchor on corporate background and risk assessments)
Collateral Raw Summary: Corporate procurement debarment results in immediate termination of public works bids, commercial pipeline freezes, and global system listing.
Collateral Firearms: Intact
Collateral Immigration: Low risk for the entity; individual officers face high deportation tracks if a parallel criminal conviction is secured
Collateral Housing: Low risk
Collateral Registry: Mandatory active listing on the GSA SAM.gov Exclusion Database
Collateral Employment: Destroys the corporation's ability to operate as a public sector vendor and bars individual principals from managing any firm engaged in public commerce.
